events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill 12 in Lebanon
events.desc: A series of Israeli strikes on vehicles in Lebanon killed at least 12 people, including a woman and her two children. The attacks targeted cars in southern Lebanon, with further reports indicating additional casualties and damage.

events.title: South Korean Vessel Attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: A South Korean vessel came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4, 2026. The incident prompted calls for Seoul to join U.S.-led operations to secure the waterway, highlighting the ongoing maritime crisis in the region.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Targeted
events.desc: At least 80 police stations and Basij paramilitary infrastructure in Iran have been damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: Pakistan's Limited War Logic
events.desc: In May 2025, Pakistan chose not to respond with long-range missile strikes to Indian attacks, opting to hold back its cruise missiles to avoid signaling nuclear escalation. This incident reflects a changing logic of conflict in South Asia, where restraint plays a key role.

events.title: UAE-Owned Tanker Leaks Fuel After Iranian Strike
events.desc: A tanker owned by the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company, struck by Iranian drones last week, has leaked a small amount of fuel off the coast of Oman. This incident highlights the ecological risks associated with the ongoing conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Israel Intensifies Strikes on Lebanon, 9 Killed
events.desc: Israel has intensified strikes on Lebanon, with the health ministry reporting nine people killed in attacks targeting cars. The military stated it attacked Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon, despite a reported ceasefire.

events.title: Israel Passes Law for Death Penalty for 7 October Attackers
events.desc: Israel has passed a law permitting the death penalty and public trials for individuals linked to the Hamas-led attacks on October 7. This legislation follows an investigation that alleged Hamas 'weaponised' sexual violence during the attacks.

events.title: Lebanon Files UN Complaint Against Iran
events.desc: Lebanon has filed a formal complaint with the United Nations against Iran, citing ongoing Iranian interference in Lebanese affairs through its proxy group, Hezbollah. This move signals a further deterioration in relations between the two countries.

events.title: U.S. and Israel Launch Military Operations Against Iran
events.desc: Large-scale military operations were launched by the United States and Israel against Iran in February. This conflict has led to daily drone attacks on Gulf Arab states, with Iran utilizing drone designs refined by Russia.

events.title: Israel and Turkey Potential Conflict
events.desc: Concerns are rising over a potential conflict between Israel and Turkey, fueled by issues related to Syria, the Palestinian territories, and regional security. Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has indirectly accused Ankara of seeking to reestablish Ottoman imperial rule.

events.title: Iran Releases Human Rights Lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on Bail
events.desc: Prominent Iranian human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh has been released on bail. She was arrested in early April amidst a crackdown during the conflict with the United States and Israel. The exact charges she faces remain unclear.

events.title: US President Trump Arrives in China for Summit
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump has landed in Beijing for a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Key topics are expected to include trade, technology, Taiwan, and the war on Iran.
